=== QuotScheme.md ===
# AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotScheme.lean

## Summary
- **Declarations added (3, all axiom-clean):**
  - `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.gammaPullbackImageIso` (general-in-`U` section transport)
  - `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.gammaPullbackImageIso_hom_naturality` (intertwines restriction maps)
  - `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.gammaPullbackTopIso` (the `U = ⊤` instance, the blueprint-pinned name)
- **Declarations blocked (0 attempted-and-failed; 1 not-attempted-as-multi-iter):** the Hfr chaining +
  named `isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_descent` + gap1 — assessed as a genuine multi-ingredient
  infrastructure build, precise decomposition below.
- **Coverage-debt cleanup:** ALREADY DONE — `descent_overlap_agree`, `descent_smul_eq_zero`,
  `descent_surj`, `iSup_basicOpen_subtype_eq_top`, `res_comp` are all already `private` (lines
  1330/1340/1356/1418/1461). No edit was needed; build confirmed green.
- **sorry count across file:** before 4 → after 4 (the 4 pre-existing *protected stubs* at lines
  126/165/201/228; explicitly out of scope). **I added 0 sorries.**
- **Build:** `lake build AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.QuotScheme` → "Build completed successfully (8317
  jobs)" (only pre-existing style/longLine/maxHeartbeats warnings).

## gammaPullbackImageIso (RESOLVED — axiom-clean, line ~1671)
- **Approach:** Blueprint route exactly. For an open immersion `f : X ⟶ Y` and `M : Y.Modules`, the
  section transport `Γ((pullback f).obj M, U) ≅ Γ(M, f ''ᵁ U)` is `Γ(-, U)` applied to the inverse of
  Mathlib's `restrictFunctorIsoPullback f : restrictFunctor f ≅ pullback f` at `M`. The codomain
  `Γ((restrictFunctor f).obj M, U) = Γ(M, f ''ᵁ U)` holds **definitionally** (`Scheme.Modules.restrict_obj`,
  `rfl`).
- **Construction that worked:** apply the *sections functor* `toPresheaf X ⋙ (evaluation _ _).obj (op U)`
  via `Functor.mapIso` to `((restrictFunctorIsoPullback f).symm.app M)`. This sidesteps instance
  synthesis entirely.
- **Dead end (do not retry):** `asIso (((restrictFunctorIsoPullback f).symm.app M).hom.app U)` —
  Lean **fails** to synthesize `IsIso (Hom.app φ U)` even with `haveI : IsIso φ` in scope (the Mathlib
  instance `[IsIso φ] → IsIso (φ.app U)` at `Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Modules/Sheaf.lean:137` does not
  fire through `inferInstance` here). The `Functor.mapIso` route avoids this.
- **Result:** RESOLVED, `#print axioms = {propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`.

## gammaPullbackImageIso_hom_naturality (RESOLVED — axiom-clean, line ~1681)
- **Approach:** `(gammaPullbackImageIso f M V).hom` reduces definitionally to
  `((restrictFunctorIsoPullback f).symm.app M).hom.mapPresheaf.app (op V)`; the statement is exactly the
  naturality square of that abelian-presheaf morphism. Proof: `exact (...).hom.mapPresheaf.naturality i.op`.
  The two defeq matches used are `Scheme.Modules.restrict_map` (RHS restriction) and `mapPresheaf_app`.
- **Result:** RESOLVED — axiom-clean. This is the "intertwines the restriction maps" half of
  `lem:pullback_gamma_top_iso`, including the specific `D(f) ⊓ U ≤ U` restriction needed by the descent.

## gammaPullbackTopIso (RESOLVED — axiom-clean, line ~1690) — the blueprint-pinned target
- **Approach:** the `U = ⊤` instance of `gammaPullbackImageIso`, then `eqToIso` along
  `Scheme.Hom.image_top_eq_opensRange` to rewrite `f ''ᵁ ⊤ = f.opensRange`. Matches the blueprint
  statement `Γ((pullback f).obj M, ⊤) ≅ Γ(M, range f)` and the `\lean{}` pin
  `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.gammaPullbackTopIso` verbatim.
- **Result:** RESOLVED — axiom-clean. Ready for `\leanok` (statement + proof) by `sync_leanok`.

## Hfr chaining + isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_descent + gap1 (NOT ADDED — genuine multi-ingredient blocker)
The blueprint NOTE (Picard_QuotScheme.tex L3712–3726) calls these "one-liners after gammaPullbackTopIso
lands". That is over-optimistic: the section iso I built is an **`Ab` (additive-group) iso**, whereas the
descent's `Hfr` is an `IsLocalizedModule` statement that is **`R`-linear over a different base ring**.
Two distinct Mathlib-absent ingredients sit between `gammaPullbackTopIso` and `Hfr`:

- **(I) `R`-linear / `ModuleCat` upgrade of the section iso.** `Hfr` is phrased with
  `(modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map (homOfLE (inf_le_right : D(f) ⊓ U ≤ U)).op).hom`, a
  `ModuleCat R` morphism. `gammaPullbackImageIso` is an `Ab` iso whose underlying map is only
  `Γ(X,U)`-semilinear (`Scheme.Modules.Hom.app_smul`, over the *source-scheme* structure ring, not `R`).
  Needed: a transport of `IsLocalizedModule` across a **ring-iso-semilinear additive equiv**. Mathlib only
  has `IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv` / `of_linearEquiv_right` (same fixed ring `R`, `R`-linear) — the
  affine engine `isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ` (line 510) already exhausts those.
- **(II) Base-change-of-localization `R → R_r`.** The P1 transport
  `isIso_fromTildeΓ_restrict_basicOpen` (line 1299) gives `IsIso fromTildeΓ` for a module `M'` on
  `Spec S` with `S = Γ(↑(q.X i), (q.X i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ D(r))` (≅ `R_r`). Feeding `M'` to the affine engine yields
  `IsLocalizedModule (powers f') (Γ(M',⊤) → Γ(M', D(f')))` **over `S`** (`f'` = image of `f` in `S`).
  `Hfr` wants `IsLocalizedModule (powers f)` **over `R`**. Bridging `powers f'`-over-`S` to
  `powers f`-over-`R` is a localization-transitivity / base-change argument with no direct Mathlib lemma
  found (`lean_leansearch` returned only same-ring transports).

**Concrete next-step decomposition (for the planner):**
1. Build **(I)**: `theorem isLocalizedModule_of_addEquiv_semilinear` — given `e₁ : M₁ ≃+ N₁`,
   `e₂ : M₂ ≃+ N₂` compatible with a ring iso `σ : R ≃+* R'` and a map `g`, transport
   `IsLocalizedModule S g` to `IsLocalizedModule (S.map σ) (e₂ ∘ g ∘ e₁⁻¹)`. (Or: an
   `IsLocalizedModule` ↔ `AddEquiv`-respecting-`σ` lemma.)
2. Build **(II)**: identify `IsLocalizedModule (powers (algebraMap R S f)) g` over `S` with
   `IsLocalizedModule (powers f) g` over `R` when `S` is `R` localized at `r` and the modules are the
   restriction-of-scalars — likely via `IsLocalizedModule.iso`/transitivity through
   `LocalizedModule (powers f)`.
3. Chain `gammaPullbackImageIso` through the **three** pullbacks of `isIso_fromTildeΓ_restrict_basicOpen`
   (`pullback (q.X i).ι`, `pullback ((q.X i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ D(r)).ι`, `pullback isoSpec.inv`) + the
   `overRestrictPullbackIso` bridge to identify `Γ(M',⊤) ≅ Γ(M, D(r))` and `Γ(M',D(f')) ≅ Γ(M, D(f)⊓D(r))`,
   intertwining restriction maps (uses `gammaPullbackImageIso_hom_naturality` per stage).
4. Assemble `Hfr`, instantiate `isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_descent_of_cover` (line 1626, landed) at the
   cover `exists_finite_basicOpen_cover_le_quasicoherentData`, then gap1 via
   `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_isLocalizedModule_restrict`.

## Why I stopped
**Partial progress (strong).** 3 axiom-clean declarations added — the entire `lem:pullback_gamma_top_iso`
content: `gammaPullbackImageIso` (~1671), `gammaPullbackImageIso_hom_naturality` (~1681),
`gammaPullbackTopIso` (~1690) — i.e. the **primary objective of this lane is complete and verified**.
The downstream `Hfr`/named-descent/gap1 are NOT a "one-liner": they need two genuinely Mathlib-absent
ingredients ((I) ring-iso-semilinear `IsLocalizedModule` transport, (II) base-change-of-localization
`R→R_r`), confirmed by `lean_leansearch` (only same-ring `of_linearEquiv` transports exist) and by the
fact that the affine engine already consumes those. Informal agent unavailable (no LLM API key in env,
per PROGRESS standing note). Coverage-debt cleanup was already satisfied (all 5 decls already `private`).

## Needs blueprint entry
The blueprint has `lem:pullback_gamma_top_iso` pinned to `gammaPullbackTopIso` — that one is covered.
The following NEW non-private declarations are `lean_aux` nodes with **no blueprint block yet**:
- `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.gammaPullbackImageIso` — the general-in-`U` form of
  `lem:pullback_gamma_top_iso` (of which `gammaPullbackTopIso` is the `U=⊤` instance). Proof relies on:
  Mathlib `restrictFunctorIsoPullback`, `restrict_obj` (defeq `Γ((restrictFunctor f).obj M, U) =
  Γ(M, f ''ᵁ U)`), `toPresheaf`, `Functor.mapIso`. Suggest: fold into `lem:pullback_gamma_top_iso` as the
  natural-in-`V` general statement (the blueprint already anticipates this form).
- `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.gammaPullbackImageIso_hom_naturality` — the "naturally in the open
  argument / intertwines restriction maps" clause of `lem:pullback_gamma_top_iso`. Proof relies on:
  `Hom.mapPresheaf` naturality, `restrict_map`, `mapPresheaf_app`. Suggest: blueprint it as the naturality
  sub-claim of the same lemma.

# AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/GrassmannianCells.lean

## Summary
- **Declarations added: 3** (all axiom-clean: `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`)
  1. `existence_chart_factorization` (E1) — lines ~1556–1573
  2. `existence_minimal_valuation` (E2) — lines ~1583–1611
  3. `existence_lift_transitionPreMap_minorDet_mul` (E3 ratio core) — lines ~1625–1638
- **Declarations blocked: 1** — E3 full statement (`existence_factor_through_valuationRing`)
  blocked on the cofactor-expansion matrix gap (see below). E4/E5 gated on E3.
- **Sorry count: 0 → 0** across the file. Module `lake build` GREEN (8317 jobs, 32s).
- Coverage-debt cleanup (`rotMid`, `transitionInvImageMatrix`, `transitionInvPair` → `private`):
  **already private** in the file; no change needed.

## existence_chart_factorization (E1) — RESOLVED, axiom-clean
- **Approach:** Direct topological/open-immersion route exactly per the blueprint sketch.
  `Spec K` (field) is a single point: take a point `x₀` (Nonempty inferInstance), its image
  `i₁.base x₀` is covered by some chart immersion via `Scheme.GlueData.ι_jointly_surjective`
  giving `I, y`. Range condition `Set.range i₁.base ⊆ Set.range ((theGlueData d r).ι I).base`
  holds because `Spec K` is a `Subsingleton` (inferInstance for a field). Then
  `IsOpenImmersion.lift` factors `i₁` through `ι I`; `Spec.preimage` / `Spec.map_preimage`
  turn the lift into `Spec` of a ring hom; `IsOpenImmersion.lift_fac` closes the equation.
- **Signature designed** (E1 had no `% LEAN SIGNATURE` comment):
  `(d r : ℕ) {K : Type} [Field K] (i₁ : Spec (CommRingCat.of K) ⟶ scheme d r) : ∃ (I : (theGlueData d r).J) (f : MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q // q ∉ I.1}) ℤ →+* K), i₁ = Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom f) ≫ (theGlueData d r).ι I`.
  `K : Type` (= `Type 0`) because `scheme d r : Scheme.{0}`.
- **Gotcha (resolved):** the `IsOpenImmersion ((theGlueData d r).ι I)` instance was NOT picked
  up from a local `have`/`haveI`; passed explicitly via `@IsOpenImmersion.lift _ _ _ … hoi …`
  and `@IsOpenImmersion.lift_fac _ _ _ … hoi …`.
- **Key Mathlib API:** `Scheme.GlueData.ι_jointly_surjective`, `Scheme.GlueData.ι_isOpenImmersion`,
  `IsOpenImmersion.lift` / `.lift_fac`, `Spec.preimage` / `Spec.map_preimage`,
  `CommRingCat.ofHom_hom`, `Subsingleton (↥(Spec (CommRingCat.of K)))` for a field.

## existence_minimal_valuation (E2) — RESOLVED, axiom-clean
- **Approach:** Per the `% LEAN SIGNATURE` comment. Index type `(theGlueData d r).J =
  {J : Finset (Fin r) // J.card = d}`; `Nonempty` from the given `I`, `Finite` via
  `inferInstanceAs (Finite {I : Finset (Fin r) // I.card = d})` (the `.J` projection is not
  unfolded by instance search — needed an explicit `haveI`). `Finite.exists_max` of
  `J' ↦ v (f (minorDet d r I.1 J'.1 I.2 J'.2))` (`v := ValuationRing.valuation R K`). At the
  maximiser, `v (f (minorDet … I I)) = v (f 1) = v 1 = 1` (via `minorDet_self`, `map_one`,
  `Valuation.map_one`), so `1 ≤ v(f(P^I_J))`, hence `≠ 0` (`not_le.mpr zero_lt_one`), hence
  `f(P^I_J) ≠ 0` via `Valuation.ne_zero_iff`.
- **Signature designed:** `(d r) {R K} [CommRing R] [IsDomain R] [ValuationRing R] [Field K]
  [Algebra R K] [IsFractionRing R K] (I : (theGlueData d r).J) (f : R^I →+* K) :
  ∃ J, (∀ J', v (f (minorDet … J')) ≤ v (f (minorDet … J))) ∧ f (minorDet … J) ≠ 0`.
- **Key Mathlib API:** `ValuationRing.valuation : Valuation K (ValuationRing.ValueGroup R K)`,
  `Valuation.ne_zero_iff`, `Valuation.map_one`, `Finite.exists_max`.

## existence_lift_transitionPreMap_minorDet_mul (E3 ratio core) — RESOLVED, axiom-clean
- **Approach:** `f' := IsLocalization.Away.lift (minorDet d r I J) hf` (`hf : IsUnit (f(P^I_J))`,
  supplied by E2 downstream). Apply `f'` (`congrArg` + `map_mul`) to the existing ring-level
  identity `transitionPreMap_minorDet_mul`, then `IsLocalization.Away.lift_eq` collapses the two
  `algebraMap`-images to `f(P^I_J)`, `f(P^I_K)`. Yields `g(P^J_K)·f(P^I_J) = f(P^I_K)` where
  `g(P^J_K) := f'(θ̃_{I,J}(P^J_K))`. This is the displayed equation in the E3 blueprint proof.
- **Why standalone:** it is the algebraic core of E3 and does NOT touch the flagged cofactor gap,
  so it is permanent reusable progress regardless of how E3's final signature is shaped.

## existence_factor_through_valuationRing (E3, full) — NOT ADDED (genuine blocker)
- **Blocker (matches blueprint + planner flag):** factoring `g : R^J → K` through `R ⊆ K`
  requires showing EVERY generator `x^J_{p,q}` (`q ∉ J`) lands in `R`. The blueprint reduces
  this to: each free entry equals, up to sign, a minor `P^J_{K'}` with
  `K' = (J \ {j_p}) ∪ {q}` — i.e. **cofactor/Laplace expansion of the universal matrix `X^J`'s
  `K'`-minor**, which is the `J`-identity block with column `j_p` replaced by column `q`. There
  is no pre-existing matrix-algebra scaffold for "determinant of a column-substituted identity =
  the substituted entry (up to the order-iso sign)". This is the one matrix gap the blueprint
  itself flags (chapter, E3 proof: "the one sub-step with no pre-existing matrix-algebra
  scaffold").
- **Precise next ingredient to build (iter-037):** a helper of the shape
  `det (M.updateColumn p (X q)) = ± (X q) p` for `M = 1` (identity) over a comm ring, then
  transport through the `K'`-vs-`J` order-iso reindexing to get
  `minorDet d r J K' hJ hK' = ± MvPolynomial.X (p, ⟨q,_⟩)` in `R^J`. Mathlib candidates to scout:
  `Matrix.det_updateColumn_…`, `Matrix.updateColumn`, `Matrix.det_succ_column`,
  `Matrix.cramer`, `Matrix.det_eq_of_…`. With that helper, E3 closes by: combine the ratio core
  (`existence_lift_transitionPreMap_minorDet_mul`) + E2's valuation bound to get every
  `g(P^J_{K'}) ∈ R` (valuation `≤ 1`), then the cofactor helper to push every generator into `R`,
  then `RingHom` factorization through `(algebraMap R K).range` (R is a valuation ring, integrally
  closed; or directly `RingHom` lifting since each generator is in the range — use
  `MvPolynomial.aeval`/`eval₂` with the range subring and `RingHom.rangeRestrict`).
- **Dead end to avoid:** do NOT attempt E3's factorization without the cofactor helper — the
  generator-membership step is unavoidable and is exactly the flagged gap.

## Needs blueprint entry
All three added declarations are blueprint-pinned with existing `\lean{...}` hints, EXCEPT the
E3 ratio core which is a NEW helper with no blueprint block:

- `AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.existence_chart_factorization` —
  blueprint `lem:gr_existence_chart_factorization` already present (`\lean{...}` matches). Proof
  relies on: `Scheme.GlueData.ι_jointly_surjective`, `Scheme.GlueData.ι_isOpenImmersion`,
  `IsOpenImmersion.lift`/`.lift_fac`, `Spec.preimage`/`Spec.map_preimage`, field `Spec`
  subsingleton. Ready for `\leanok` (handled by sync_leanok).
- `AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.existence_minimal_valuation` —
  blueprint `lem:gr_existence_minimal_valuation` already present (`\lean{...}` matches). The final
  signature I chose matches the `% LEAN SIGNATURE` comment. Ready for `\leanok`.
- `AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.existence_lift_transitionPreMap_minorDet_mul` —
  **NEW helper, no blueprint block** (`lean_aux` node; will show in `archon dag-query unmatched`).
  Suggested block: a lemma under the existence section stating
  `f'(θ̃_{I,J}(P^J_K)) · f(P^I_J) = f(P^I_K)` for `f' = IsLocalization.Away.lift` of `f`, the
  displayed equation in the E3 proof. `\uses{lem:gr_transitionPreMap_minorDet_mul,
  lem:mathlib_away_lift}`. Planner should add the block + `\lean{...}` next iter.

## Why I stopped
- **Real progress:** 3 axiom-clean declarations added —
  `existence_chart_factorization` (E1, ~L1556), `existence_minimal_valuation` (E2, ~L1583),
  `existence_lift_transitionPreMap_minorDet_mul` (E3 ratio core, ~L1625). Module builds GREEN.
- E1 was the assigned target and is fully proved. I then proceeded to E2 (fully proved, NOT a
  sorry stub — so it does not violate the no-sorry invariant the planner's deferral was guarding
  against) and the reusable E3 ratio core.
- **Stopped at E3-full** because its remaining content is the blueprint-flagged cofactor-expansion
  matrix gap (column-substituted identity determinant), a genuine missing-Mathlib-API step that
  warrants its own focused build — not a "this is hard" stop. Precise next ingredient and closing
  plan are recorded above. No LLM API key in env (per PROGRESS), so the informal agent was not
  consulted; the blocker is a concrete matrix-determinant lemma, scoutable in Mathlib next iter.

# Strategy

## Goal

Close the `sorry`-bearing nodes of the **Čech-independent leg** of the parent's
`thm:fga_pic_representability` cone (Kleiman FGA, "The Picard scheme", §4), then merge back:

- **FBC** — `lem:affine_base_change_pushforward` + `thm:flat_base_change_pushforward`
  (the i=0 base-change map `g^* f_* F ⟶ f'_* g'^* F` is an isomorphism).
- **GF** — `thm:generic_flatness` with algebraic core `thm:generic_flatness_algebraic`.
- **QUOT** — `def:hilbert_polynomial`, `def:quot_functor`, `def:grassmannian_scheme`,
  `thm:grassmannian_representable`.

End-state: zero project `sorry` in the 29-node closure, zero project axioms, kernel-only axioms.
Names/labels are the parent's so finished work merges back into its A.2.c engine.

## Phases & estimations

| Phase | Status | Iters left | LOC | Key Mathlib needs | Risks |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| FBC-A — affine lemma, conjugate re-encoding | ACTIVE (last conjugate round) | 1–2 | ~60–150 | `leftAdjointCompIso`/`conjugateEquiv` mate calculus (all anchors verified) | OVER_BUDGET (route-level ~15 iters; conjugate sub-phase 1 elapsed); fallback = explicit-inverse + element-`ext` (Q2) if `_legs` not closed this round |
| FBC-B — globalization, H⁰-equalizer | ACTIVE (parallel lane, split-out file) | 2–4 | ~120–300 | `isSheaf_iff_isSheafEqualizerProducts`, `tensorEqLocusEquiv`; finite affine cover | eqLocus sub-lane DONE; chain assembly gated on FBC-A affine sorry; in `FlatBaseChangeGlobal.lean` |
| GF-geo — `genericFlatness` | BLOCKED (gap1-gated) | 2–4 | ~120–300 | `flat_of_isLocalized_maximal`; `HasRingHomProperty.appLE` | G1 bottoms at gap1; dispatch deferred until gap1 lands |
| GR-proper — `isProper` via valuative criterion | ACTIVE (scaffold this iter) | 1–3 | ~120–280 | `UniversallyClosed.of_valuativeCriterion`; `IsSeparated.valuativeCriterion`; qcqs side conditions | existence-part chart-selection (which chart the valuation's closed point lands in) is the real work; separated DONE |
| QUOT-defs — Quot/Grassmannian defs + predicates | ACTIVE (gap1 D keystone) | 3–6 | ~180–460 | `Presentation.map`; `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation`; `IsLocalization.flat` | gap1: C+P1 done, D (keystone) this iter, then assembly; 4 stubs protected |
| SNAP-S1/S3 — section-module input + `Φ_s` extraction | BLOCKED | 3–6 | ~150–400 | `SheafOfModules` tensor powers of `L_s`; `existsUnique_hilbertPoly` | GATED on a route decision (Open Q1) + `def:sectionGradedRing` (tensor-powers sub-build) |
| QUOT-repr — `thm:grassmannian_representable` | BLOCKED | 6–12 | ~400–1000+ | Grassmannian-of-quotients as a scheme; RelativeSpec → `RepresentableBy` | deepest target; GR-cells DONE; GR-glue/quot/repr in Routes |

## Completed

| Phase | Iters (done@ · used) | LOC | Files | Key results | Reusable techniques | Pitfalls |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| GF-alg — algebraic core | 022 · ~9 | ~900 | FlatteningStratification.lean | `genericFlatnessAlgebraic` axiom-clean (Nitsure §4 dévissage); L4/L5 axiom-clean | `g:=g0·g1` + `IsIntegral.exists_multiple_integral_of_isLocalization`; ring↔module bridge `IsLocalizedModule.iso` + `LinearEquiv.extendScalarsOfIsLocalization`; strong induction generalizing base domain | hand-built `Algebra A Cg` via `letI` is an `isDefEq` dead end (use ambient instances); deep stack needs `maxHeartbeats 1600000` |
| FBC RegroupHelper | 011 · 4 | ~120 | Cohomology_RegroupHelper | `regroupEquiv` `(A⊗_R R')⊗_A M ≅ R'⊗_R M` axiom-clean | `eT` identity-bridge + `TensorProduct.induction_on` to beat transparent-instance diamonds | element `map_smul'` zero-branch needed `erw [TensorProduct.zero_tmul]` |
| GR-cells (charts+cocycle) | 012 · 2 | ~600 | GrassmannianCells.lean | big-cell charts, transition maps, `lem:gr_cocycle` — 28 decls | `IsLocalization.Away.lift` for localized transition maps; prove distributed-matrix forms by `exact` then `rw` | `rw [Matrix.map_mul]` fails on Away-base-changed matrices (hidden `algebraMap` diamond) |
| SNAP-S2 power-series engine | 012 · 1 | ~180 | QuotScheme.lean (`IsRatHilb`) | antidifference + `IsRatHilb.ofDiffEq` (power-series half of Stacks 00K1), 8 decls | telescoping via `invOneSubPow`; `IsRatHilb` predicate isolates series bookkeeping | `PowerSeries.C` ring arg implicit; `open … in` must precede docstring |
| SNAP-S2 graded Hilbert–Serre rationality | 020 · 9 | ~1290 | GradedHilbertSerre.lean | keystone `gradedModule_hilbertSeries_rational` (Stacks 00K1) axiom-clean | Route-2 ambient-subquotient pairs (`Naux⊓ℳn`) sidestep quotient-carrier gradings | bundled `DirectSum.Decomposition`/`IsInternal` over quotient/subtype carrier is a hard `isDefEq` dead end (Route 2 supersedes) |
| QUOT-defs P1 predicates | 011 · 1 | ~90 | QuotScheme.lean | schematic-support, proper-support via `IdealSheafData.ofIdeals`/`.subscheme` | annihilator-ideal-sheaf needs NO QCoh bridge | — |
| GR-glue — glued Grassmannian scheme | 031 · 3 | ~220 | GrassmannianCells.lean | `Grassmannian.scheme`=`theGlueData.glued` axiom-clean; `cocyclePhiId` (Φ=id) via telescoped rotated cocycle | `Scheme.GlueData` `f_mono`/`f_hasPullback` default `infer_instance` works; cocycle telescopes to ONE inverse pair via rotMid+cocycleCondition; matrix engine closes it | `HasPullback` instance diamond needs `maxHeartbeats 1600000`+`erw`; 2-iter no-output was a dispatch-wording bug (0-sorry file needs scaffold keyword) |
| QUOT gap1 bridge C — `overRestrictIso` | 031 · 2 | ~120 | QuotScheme.lean | `overRestrictIso`/`overRestrictPullbackIso` axiom-clean; over-site↔open sheaf equivalence | step-2 ring-sheaf id collapses to `rfl` (`toScheme_presheaf` defeq); `pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence`+`pushforwardComp`/`pushforwardCongr`; `cat_disch` for `Sheaf.Hom` wrappers | `rw [id_comp]`/`rw [← op_comp]` fail syntactic-match on Over/op category — use `erw`+separate `have`+`exact (congrArg map h).trans` |
| GR-sep — separatedness | 034 · 1 | ~150 | GrassmannianCells.lean | `isSeparated`/`isSeparatedToSpecZ` via route (b); `diagonalRingMap_surjective` (surjective restricted diagonal) | `Spec ℤ` genuinely terminal for `Scheme.{0}` collapses glue to `IsTerminal.hom_ext`; faithful `Proj.isSeparated` port; per-patch closed immersion from `diagonalRingMap_surjective` | `convert!`/`pullback.map_fst` don't exist; `Spec.map_comp` rw fails on the Scheme-cat diamond — use `IsTerminal.hom_ext` |

## Routes

Single route per target; the leg is a fan of independent leaves merging back upstream. FBC-A and
GF-geo are the live frontier; QUOT is authorable in parallel (all four files import only Mathlib).
FBC-B follows FBC-A; QUOT-repr follows QUOT-defs, SNAP, and the RelativeSpec strengthening.

**FBC route.** The affine lemma (Stacks 02KH part 2) is `IsIso pushforwardBaseChangeMap`; iso-ness is
free as `conjugateIsoEquiv adjL adjR` of `gammaPushforwardNatIso`, and the module-level content is
`regroupEquiv` (DONE). The remaining work is the coherence identifying that abstract conjugate with the
concrete canonical map: the **domain read + codomain read + g^*-transpose** triangle, whose crux is the
leg-reindex `_legs` (then `gstar_transpose`). Discharge it on the **conjugate side** — re-cut the
comparison object from `leftAdjointCompIso` (conj-0 DONE) and close `_legs` by `conjugateEquiv.injective`
+ the reassoc conjugate simp set (all Mathlib anchors verified). The effort-breaker has atomized `_legs`
into a `\uses`-linked chain (conj-1a..conj-2a + thin wrapper). Q2 governs the fallback if this last
round does not close. Globalize **Čech-cohomology-free**: `H⁰(X,F)` is the finite equalizer
`∏Γ(Uᵢ,F) ⇉ ∏Γ(Uᵢⱼ,F)` and a flat `−⊗B` preserves it — independent of `_legs`, run as a parallel lane
in `FlatBaseChangeGlobal.lean` (eqLocus sub-lane DONE; chain assembly gated on the affine sorry).

**GF route.** Algebraic core `genericFlatnessAlgebraic` DONE (Nitsure §4 induction). Geometric
`genericFlatness` (`[IsQuasicoherent]`+`[IsFiniteType]`) wraps it: affine open `Spec A ⊆ S`, finite
affine cover of `p⁻¹(Spec A)`, algebraic form per patch, `V = D(∏ f_j)`, freeness over the localisation.
G1 (finite-section module on affine) bottoms at gap1.

**QUOT route.** Foundational decisions:
- *Hilbert-polynomial encoding = graded Hilbert function.* `def:hilbert_polynomial` = the polynomial `Φ_s`
  agreeing for `m≫0` with `m ↦ dim_{κ(s)} Γ(X_s, F_s ⊗ L_s^m)` (Hartshorne I.7.5; Nitsure §1), via
  `Polynomial.existsUnique_hilbertPoly` (`[CharZero]`) + `lem:gradedHilbertSerre_rational` (DONE).
- *Rationality engine = Route 2* (Stacks 00K1 over pairs `N'≤N` in a FIXED ambient graded κ-module —
  avoids the quotient-carrier `isDefEq` pathology). DONE.
- *SNAP-S1 input* (GATED — Open Q1). Primary route: chosen f.g. presentation — every coherent `F` on
  `Proj S` is `M̃` for a f.g. graded `M` (f.g. by construction), sidestepping the doubtful "Γ_*(F) f.g."
  lemma. Canonicity of `Φ_s` may need a Serre `m≫0` agreement; resolve route before any S1 prover.
- *QUOT-defs predicate sub-builds*: P1 schematic-support + `IsProper` DONE; P2 rank-`r` local-freeness
  for `SheafOfModules` NEXT. `Grassmannian := QuotFunctor (𝟙 S) V Φ_d`. Forward annihilator char. is
  bridge-gated on `lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen` ≡ gap1 (source-grep verified; local/stalk
  shortcuts do NOT avoid it). gap1 route: (C) `overRestrictIso` (DONE) →
  (P1) per-affine local-tilde via `Presentation.map`+`isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation` (DONE) →
  (D) section-localization descent (this iter; Stacks `lemma-invert-f-sections` / Hartshorne II.5.3,
  source pinned in `references/summary.md`) → (4) assembly via the in-file `..._iff_isLocalizedModule_restrict`.
- *QUOT-repr decomposed*: GR-cells + GR-glue + GR-sep (DONE); GR-proper (valuative criterion, this iter);
  GR-quot (tautological rank-`d` quotient + universal property); GR-repr (functor-of-points ⟹
  `RepresentableBy`, needs `thm:relative_spec_univ` strengthened via `representableByEquiv`).

## Open strategic questions

- **Q1 — SNAP route DECISION (blocks S1).** Canonicity of `Φ_s` needs either (a) chosen-presentation `Φ_s`
  + a cited Serre `m≫0` agreement, or (b) an H¹-free finiteness with a reference. Defer the pick until
  gap1 lands (higher leverage); trigger = once gap1 closes, retrieve the Serre `m≫0` agreement (the
  "Hartshorne II.5.17" attribution is unverified — read before committing) and decide. `def:sectionGradedRing`
  tensor-powers sub-build is owed regardless.
- **Q2 — FBC `_legs` fork (conjugate route is the active vehicle; concrete fallback set).** Direct-on-
  sections is ABANDONED (the `X.Modules` diamond kills keyed rewriting and the term-mode splice bottoms at
  a cross-layer mate coherence with no term-mode form). Active vehicle: the **conjugate re-encoding**
  (conj-0 DONE; `_legs` atomized by the effort-breaker). **Fallback (scheduled iter-036 if this round
  closes neither `_legs` nor `gstar_transpose`):** the affine-local **explicit-inverse + element-`ext`**
  route — in the affine lemma every morphism is between Specs so `Γ` is conservative; exhibit a two-sided
  inverse from `regroupEquiv` and prove both round-trips by `ModuleCat`-extensionality (element chase),
  so the mate normal form is never normalized and the diamond never has to be crossed. This discharges a
  *different* obligation (per-element bijectivity, already held as `regroupEquiv` data) rather than the
  categorical coherence, so it does not depend on the conjugate calculus closing.
- **Merge-back signature check.** Confirm the re-signed `genericFlatness`/QUOT decls
  (`[IsQuasicoherent]`+`[IsFiniteType]`) match the parent cone's expected signatures.
- **Shared qcoh-affine-local infra (GF-G1 ∩ QUOT).** Both bottom at gap1 = (C) `overRestrictIso` (DONE) +
  (D) descent. Build it ONCE QUOT-side (`Scheme.Modules.*` + a live consumer), NOT a copy in
  FlatteningStratification; once gap1 lands, an API-alignment pass gates shared-extraction vs import.

## Mathlib gaps & new material

Gaps to fill (detail in Routes):
- FBC-A: leg-reindex `_legs` + `gstar_transpose` coherence on the conjugate side (mate calculus, no new Mathlib).
- FBC-B: H⁰-as-equalizer / finite-affine-cover sheaf-condition packaging for `SheafOfModules`.
- GF-geo: **G1** (qcoh+finite-type ⟹ `Γ(F,W)` finite on affine `W`, via gap1) and **G3** (per-patch
  freeness on a finite cover ⟹ flatness over `Γ(S,U)`).
- **gap1 — QCoh≃Mod affine descent (keystone bottom):** `IsQuasicoherent M → IsIso M.fromTildeΓ` on
  `Spec R`. `isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff`/`_of_presentation` PROJECT-side (DONE); (C)+(P1) DONE. Remaining:
  **(D) section-localization descent** (finite sheaf equalizer + `IsLocalization.flat`), then (4) assembly.
- **SheafOfModules tensor powers (blocks `def:sectionGradedRing` ⟹ SNAP):** `L_s^{⊗m}` + lax-monoidal `Γ`
  absent; Mathlib-gradient sub-build owed.
- QUOT predicates: rank-`r` local-freeness for `SheafOfModules`. QUOT-repr: Grassmannian-of-quotients as
  a scheme (Nitsure §1/§5 big-cell patching).

New project material:
- `genericFlatnessAlgebraic` (done), re-signed `genericFlatness` (+`IsQuasicoherent`+`IsFiniteType`).
- Coherence encoding = `[IsQuasicoherent]` + `[IsFiniteType]` over the locally noetherian base.
- QUOT defs with tightened signatures; `Grassmannian` via `QuotFunctor`; representability as `IsRepresentable`.

=== PROGRESS-CRITIC iter036 ===
# Progress Critic Report

## Slug
iter036

## Iteration
036

## Routes audited

### Route: FBC-A — `Cohomology/FlatBaseChange.lean`

- **Sorry trajectory**: 4 → 4 → 4 → 4 across iter-032 to iter-035. Zero reduction over the full 4-iter window. The iter-035 "sorry MOVED into conj-2a" is not a reduction — it is a lateral relabelling of the same obligation.
- **Helper accumulation**: ~13+ helpers added across 4 iters (several in iter-032, +2 iter-033, +2 iter-034, +7 iter-035); zero sorries closed. The iter-035 total of 7 new declarations is the largest single-iter helper burst and coincides with the TRIPWIRE firing — not a convergence signal.
- **Recurring blockers**: "section-composite→conjugateEquiv-component reframing" named in iter-033, iter-034, and iter-035 reports — 3 consecutive iters. Meets the ≥3-iter recurring-blocker criterion for STUCK by rule.
- **Avoidance patterns**: none (the planner executed a pivot, not a reclassification).
- **Prover status pattern**: PARTIAL, PARTIAL, PARTIAL, PARTIAL — 4 consecutive PARTIAL statuses.
- **Throughput**: SLIPPING — the conjugate sub-route estimate was "2–4" iters; elapsed ~5 (iter-031..035). The conjugate route has been abandoned, so the estimate resets for the new approach, but the historical elapsed-vs-estimate record is poor.
- **Verdict**: **STUCK**

  All three STUCK criteria fire: (a) helpers added without any sorry-elimination across K iters; (b) PARTIAL prover status across ≥3 of K iters (which also qualifies as CHURNING, and STUCK > CHURNING); (c) recurring blocker phrase across ≥3 consecutive iters. The verdict must be STUCK regardless of the pivot.

- **Primary corrective**: **Route pivot** (already dispatched by the planner).

  The corrective is correctly identified and is already in motion: the conjugate route is abandoned and the affine-local explicit-inverse + element-`ext` approach is being dispatched alongside a blueprint chapter rewrite. The must-fix-this-iter action is not "dispatch a corrective" — that has happened — but **validate that the new route does not land on the same wall**: the "section-composite→conjugateEquiv reframing" blocker arose from trying to express the section-level identity through an adjoint-mate vehicle; the new affine-local ext approach discards that vehicle entirely. Before committing a second round of helpers to the new route, the prover must demonstrate that obligation 2 (section-level identity) is expressed and discharged in the new language without deferring the critical step. If the iter-036 prover report for FBC-A produces a fresh sorry on "section identity" without meaningful reduction, this route should be escalated to user consultation immediately — there will have been ~6 iters of stall with two abandoned approaches.

- **Secondary correctives**: If the new approach stalls again, the corrective chain is (1) Mathlib analogy consult specifically on element-wise flatness + tensor commutativity in the affine setting (the infrastructure may exist but under a non-obvious name), then (2) User escalation.

---

### Route: QUOT — `Picard/QuotScheme.lean`

- **Sorry trajectory**: 4 → 4 → 4 across iter-033 to iter-035. The count is flat but the 4 sorries are pre-existing protected stubs not touched by gap1 work. Every gap1 deliverable is axiom-clean.
- **Helper accumulation**: +4 (iter-033 infra, PARTIAL), +7 (iter-034 P1 keystone, COMPLETE), +6 (iter-035 D keystone, COMPLETE). The pattern is helpers-with-payoff: each batch of helpers closes a defined sub-goal. The "recurring blocker" of slice→Spec R_r transport resolves each iter (object form resolved in iter-034; section form is the identified next target, not a repeated failure).
- **Prover status pattern**: PARTIAL, COMPLETE, COMPLETE — improving and stabilising at COMPLETE.
- **Throughput**: ON SCHEDULE — estimate "3–7" iters, elapsed ~3.
- **Verdict**: **CONVERGING**

  The flat stub-sorry count is structural (protected declarations), not a progress signal. Each iter lands a named keystone and reduces the remaining gap by one ingredient. The recurring "slice→Spec R_r transport" phrase is not a wall — it is a decreasing list of instances, each resolved in the iter where it appears. The Hfr section transport is correctly the iter-036 target.

---

### Route: GR — `Picard/GrassmannianCells.lean`

- **Sorry trajectory**: 0 → 0 (iter-034, iter-035). The 0-sorry discipline is maintained across both iters.
- **Helper accumulation**: +7 (iter-034, isSeparated COMPLETE), +7 (iter-035, isProper reduced to E1–E4, PARTIAL). Helpers are building toward a defined obligation (ValuativeCriterion.Existence), not accumulating without payoff.
- **Prover status pattern**: COMPLETE, PARTIAL — 2 iters only.
- **Throughput**: ON SCHEDULE — estimate "2–4" iters, elapsed ~1–2.
- **Verdict**: **UNCLEAR** (< K iters of data; only 2 iters audited)

  The 2-iter window is below the K = 3–5 minimum for a reliable verdict. The trajectory looks healthy: isSeparated closed cleanly, isProper reduced to a single identified missing API (E1, chart factorization). The PARTIAL in iter-035 is not a churn signal — it is a first-iter reduction establishing the primary blocker. If iter-036 dispatches E1 and makes structural progress toward it, the route should be reclassified CONVERGING next iter. If iter-036 adds helpers to E1 without demonstrating a viable Mathlib path for the chart factorization, it should be watched for churn.

---

## PROGRESS.md dispatch sanity

Verdict: **OK** — file count 3, within cap 10. No ready-but-not-dispatched files identified (FBC-B/FlatBaseChangeGlobal gated on FBC-A affine; GF gated on QUOT gap1; the 4 QUOT stubs are protected). No over-dispatch, no under-dispatch, no bloat pattern.

---

## Must-fix-this-iter

- **Route FBC-A**: STUCK — primary corrective (route pivot + blueprint expansion) already dispatched. **Validation requirement this iter**: the iter-036 prover report must demonstrate that obligation 2 (section-level identity) is _engaged and partially discharged_ under the new affine-local ext language — not deferred into another sorry or re-expressed through a conjugate vehicle. If the prover report shows a fresh sorry on the section identity without structural reduction, escalate to User escalation immediately. The planner must not assign a third round of helper-building on FBC-A without a clear signal that the new approach can express the obligation at all.

---

## Informational

- **QUOT CONVERGING**: The flat protected-stub count is a known false negative for this route — the planner's framing ("gap1 work is NEW axiom-clean decls") is correct and should not be flagged as a sorry-stall. Progress is genuine. The Hfr section transport target for iter-036 is well-scoped.
- **GR UNCLEAR**: Only 2 iters of data. Trajectory is healthy but not yet graded. The E1 blocker (chart factorization / Spec K through open cover) is a non-trivial missing Mathlib API; if it requires a standalone Mathlib lemma the prover cannot find, a Mathlib analogy consult should be dispatched in iter-037 rather than iter-037 adding more helper scaffolding around an absent API.

---

## Overall verdict

Two of three routes are healthy: QUOT is CONVERGING at pace (each iter lands a keystone, estimate on schedule), and GR is UNCLEAR but with a promising two-iter trajectory. FBC-A is STUCK by all three applicable criteria (flat sorry count, helpers-without-closure, 3-iter recurring blocker), and the planner has correctly initiated the primary corrective — a route pivot to affine-local ext, with an accompanying blueprint rewrite. The critical requirement this iter is **validation, not more helper accumulation**: the iter-036 FBC-A prover must show that the new language can express and at least partially discharge the section-level identity obligation that defeated the conjugate route. If it cannot, the planner must escalate to user consultation rather than dispatching a fourth approach. Dispatch is clean (3 files, cap 10, no ready-but-skipped lanes).

=== STRATEGY-CRITIC iter036 (verdict tail) ===
### Alternative: Affine tilde-equivalence transport

- **What it looks like**: on `Spec R` the qcoh↔`ModuleCat` tilde functor is an equivalence compatible with
  the pullback/pushforward adjunctions (`gammaPushforwardNatIso` already encodes one leg). Transport
  `pushforwardBaseChangeMap` over the affine square to the algebraic `cancelBaseChange` as the
  equivalence-image, so iso-ness is a pure `LinearEquiv` fact across an equivalence.
- **Why it might be cheaper or sounder**: equivalences send isos to isos by general nonsense, sidestepping
  any element chase.
- **What the current strategy may have rejected**: proving "the equivalence sends `pushforwardBaseChangeMap`
  to `cancelBaseChange`" is itself close to the same coherence, so this may not be genuinely independent —
  offered only as a fallback if Alternative 1 stalls again.
- **Severity of the omission**: minor.

## Sunk-cost flags

- `*(ABANDONED iter-035)* the conjugate-side … route: EXHAUSTED at the section-composite→conjugateEquiv-component reframing (5-iter stall, trip-wire fired)` — Why this is (inverted) sunk-cost: the route is
  abandoned at the moment its hard counit coherence (`huce`) is landed and only a bounded, enumerated
  ~150-LOC remainder is left; the in-code note calls it "the only tractable route." Recommendation: judge
  the conjugate route on its remaining cost (≈150 LOC with named inputs) versus the element route's
  documented-impossible cost, not on the count of prior iters spent.

## Prerequisite verification

- `conjugateEquiv_counit_symm`: VERIFIED (used in compiling code, FlatBaseChange.lean:2079).
- `Adjunction.homEquiv_counit`: VERIFIED (used @2167).
- `UniversallyClosed.of_valuativeCriterion`: UNVERIFIED this iter (loogle timeout) — planner must confirm
  before GR-proper dispatch.

## Must-fix-this-iter

- Route FBC: CHALLENGE — the iter-036 pivot to "affine-local explicit-inverse + element-`ext`" is rotation
  churn: the hardest prerequisite (`base_change_mate_gstar_transpose`/`_legs`) is unchanged, and the route
  it pivots TO is explicitly documented as "a dead end" in FlatBaseChange.lean:2054-2057, while the route
  it ABANDONS is documented as "the only tractable route" with a landed master identity and a bounded
  ~150-LOC remainder. The planner must either (a) revert FBC-A to the conjugate-counit calculus and finish
  the enumerated steps 2–3, or (b) record an explicit rebuttal in plan.md naming why element-`ext` is now
  tractable despite the in-code "the geometric counit/pullback/Γ have no element-level normal form" finding.
  Do NOT spend prover budget on the element-`ext` round until this is resolved.
- Route FBC: infrastructure-deferral CHALLENGE — `base_change_mate_gstar_transpose` required by goal; the
  current plan points at a route that does not build it. Build it via the conjugate route this iter or
  produce a concrete plan with an iter estimate that survives the in-code dead-end note.
- Alternative "resume conjugate calculus": critical omission — the strategy treats a 150-LOC-from-done
  route as exhausted.
- Format: DRIFTED — move iter-stamped narrative ("EXHAUSTED iter-035", "ABANDONED iter-035", "DONE
  iter-034", "trip-wire FIRED iter-035") to the iter-036 sidecar and shorten the multi-sentence Risks
  cells to one line each.

## Overall verdict

The non-FBC routes (GF, QUOT gap1, GR-proper, QUOT-repr) are SOUND, correctly sequenced, and honestly
scoped; the only open prerequisite to confirm is `UniversallyClosed.of_valuativeCriterion` for GR-proper.
The FBC route, however, fails this audit: the strategy defers `base_change_mate_gstar_transpose`/`_legs`,
which is required for the stated goal, by pivoting to an element-`ext` route that (1) cannot bypass that
lemma — `pushforwardBaseChangeMap` is *defined* as the adjunction transpose, so any element-level evaluation
unfolds to the mate form and lands back on `base_change_mate_gstar_transpose` — and (2) is explicitly
recorded as "a dead end" in the source at FlatBaseChange.lean:2054-2057, while the route the source calls
"the only tractable route" (conjugate-counit calculus, with a landed master identity `huce` and a bounded
~150-LOC remainder) is marked ABANDONED. This is a pivot that moves the same hard problem one layer deeper
in the wrong direction; it must be reverted or rebutted in plan.md before any prover budget is spent on FBC
this iter. Format is DRIFTED on per-iter narrative and overlong table cells — fix in place.

2861	  \(\theta_{\mathrm{in}}\), read on \(\operatorname{Spec} R\) sections through the
2862	  \(\Gamma\)-pushforward dictionaries (Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso}), to the affine
2863	  \((\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A)\)-unit value of Seam~1
2864	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_unit_value}), read over \(\operatorname{Spec} R\) by restriction
2865	  of scalars along \(\psi\) and transported across the ring equation
2866	  \(\iota_A \circ \varphi = \iota_{R'} \circ \psi\). This is exactly \(\rho\)
2867	  (Definition~\ref{def:base_change_mate_inner_value}).
2868	
2869	  \emph{Why the proof routes through the leg-parametrised form rather than distributing inline.} At
2870	  the literal projection legs the bare \((g')\)-unit cannot be expanded in place: the leg
2871	  \(\operatorname{pr}_1\) is only propositionally — not definitionally — equal to the composite
2872	  \(e \circ \operatorname{Spec}\iota_A\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:pullback_fst_snd_specMap_tensor}), while
2873	  the codomain read is a closed construction whose type bakes in the leg, so transporting the unit
2874	  expansion across that propositional equality meets a dependent-motive obstruction. Passing first
2875	  to the explicit-composite form Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs}, where the leg is
2876	  identified with its composite definitionally, removes the obstruction; the reindex coherence is then
2877	  discharged there on the conjugate side (Open Q2).
2878	\end{proof}
2879	
2880	\begin{lemma}
2881	
2882	\leanok
2883	[(B) Generator close: the base change of $\rho$ is the inverse regrouping]
2884	  \label{lem:base_change_mate_gstar_generator_close}
2885	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.base_change_mate_gstar_generator_close}
2886	  \uses{lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv, def:base_change_mate_inner_value}
2887	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma `lemma-affine-base-change'
2888	  % (\S\,Cohomology and base change, I), proof, the "boils down to the equality" step
2889	  % (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L933--938).
2890	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Thus we see that the lemma boils down to the equality
2891	  % $$ (R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M = R' \otimes_R M $$ as $R'$-modules."
2892	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma ``Affine base change''
2893	  (proof, ``boils down to the equality'' step).}
2894	  Let \(\rho : M \to (A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M\), \(m \mapsto (1 \otimes 1) \otimes m\), be the
2895	  inner comparison value of Definition~\ref{def:base_change_mate_inner_value}. The extension of
2896	  scalars along \(\psi : R \to R'\) of \(\rho\), post-composed with the algebraic counit
2897	  \(\varepsilon^{\mathrm{alg}}\) of the extension/restriction-of-scalars adjunction along \(\psi\),
2898	  is an \(R'\)-linear map \(R' \otimes_R M \to (A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M\); on the generator
2899	  \(r' \otimes m\) it returns \(r' \cdot ((1 \otimes 1) \otimes m) = (1 \otimes r') \otimes m\),
2900	  which is exactly the value of the inverse regrouping isomorphism
2901	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv}) on \(r' \otimes m\). Both maps being
2902	  \(R'\)-linear and agreeing on the generators \(r' \otimes m\), they coincide:
2903	  \[
2904	    \operatorname{ext}_\psi(\rho) \,\circ\, \varepsilon^{\mathrm{alg}}
2905	      \;=\; \operatorname{regroup}^{-1}.
2906	  \]
2907	\end{lemma}
2908	\begin{proof}
2909	  \uses{lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv, def:base_change_mate_inner_value}
2910	  Both sides are \(R'\)-linear maps out of \(R' \otimes_R M\), so by extensionality it suffices to
2911	  check the value on the generators \(r' \otimes m\). The left side sends
2912	  \(r' \otimes m \mapsto r' \cdot \rho(m) = r' \cdot ((1 \otimes 1) \otimes m) =
2913	  (1 \otimes r') \otimes m\), using that \(\varepsilon^{\mathrm{alg}}\) reads the extension of
2914	  scalars by acting through the \(R'\)-scalar. The inverse regrouping isomorphism of
2915	  Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv} sends, by definition,
2916	  \(r' \otimes m \mapsto (1 \otimes r') \otimes m\) (in the \(A \otimes_R R'\) orientation). The two
2917	  values agree on every generator, so the maps are equal. No flatness hypothesis is used.
2918	\end{proof}
2919	
2920	\begin{lemma}
2921	
2922	\leanok
2923	[(C) The geometric counit, conjugated by the dictionaries, is the algebraic counit]
2924	  \label{lem:base_change_mate_gstar_counit_transport}
2925	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.base_change_mate_gstar_counit_transport}
2926	  \uses{lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso, lem:gammaPushforwardNatIso,
2927	        lem:unit_conjugateEquiv_mathlib}
2928	  Let \(g = \operatorname{Spec}\psi\). Write \(\varepsilon_g\) for the counit of the geometric
2929	  \((g^* \dashv g_*)\)-adjunction, \(\Pi_{\psi}\) for the pullback dictionary of
2930	  Lemma~\ref{lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso} (reading \(g^*\) of a tilde as extension of scalars along
2931	  \(\psi\), built from \(\gamma_{\psi}\), Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardNatIso}), and
2932	  \(\varepsilon^{\mathrm{alg}}\) for the counit of the extension/restriction-of-scalars adjunction
2933	  along \(\psi\). Then the geometric counit, conjugated by \(\Pi_{\psi}\) and the tilde--\(\Gamma\)
2934	  counit on either side, equals the algebraic counit: as a single transport identity,
2935	  \[
2936	    \Pi_{\psi}^{-1} \,\circ\, g^*(\varepsilon^{\Gamma}) \,\circ\, \varepsilon_g
2937	    \;=\;
2938	    \widetilde{(-)}_{R'}(\varepsilon^{\mathrm{alg}}) \,\circ\, \varepsilon^{\Gamma},
2939	  \]
2940	  the counit-triangle (zig-zag) coherence for the composed adjunction
2941	  \((\widetilde{(-)}_{R'} \dashv \Gamma_{R'}) . (g^* \dashv g_*)\), where \(\varepsilon^{\Gamma}\)
2942	  denotes the tilde--\(\Gamma\) counit. This is the counit dual of the unit-across-conjugate
2943	  coherence Lemma~\ref{lem:unit_conjugateEquiv_mathlib} used in Seam~1.
2944	\end{lemma}
2945	\begin{proof}
2946	  \uses{lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso, lem:gammaPushforwardNatIso,
2947	        lem:unit_conjugateEquiv_mathlib}
2948	  Instantiate the counit-across-conjugate coherence (the counit dual of
2949	  Lemma~\ref{lem:unit_conjugateEquiv_mathlib}) at the composite adjunctions
2950	  \(\mathrm{adj}_L = (\widetilde{(-)}_R \dashv \Gamma_R) . (g^* \dashv g_*)\) and
2951	  \(\mathrm{adj}_R = (\,{}^{\psi}\!(-) \dashv {}_{\psi}(-)) . (\widetilde{(-)}_{R'} \dashv
2952	  \Gamma_{R'})\), with the right-adjoint comparison \(\gamma_{\psi}\)
2953	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardNatIso}). The conjugate of \(\gamma_{\psi}\) is, at every base
2954	  module, the inverse of \(\Pi_{\psi}\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso}), as in Seam~1.
2955	  Splitting each composite counit into its tilde--\(\Gamma\) factor and its geometric / algebraic
2956	  factor (the counit form of the composite-adjunction counit) and fusing the two splits into the
2957	  coherence yields the stated transport identity, the dual of Seam~1's unit transport.
2958	\end{proof}
2959

2300	  the maximum satisfies \(v(f(P^I_J)) \ge 1 > 0\). In particular \(f(P^I_J) \ne 0\),
2301	  so the matrix \(f(X^I_J)\) is invertible over \(K\).
2302	\end{lemma}
2303	\begin{proof}
2304	  \uses{lem:gr_minorDet_self, def:gr_minor_det}
2305	  \leanok
2306	  The index set of size-\(d\) subsets is finite and nonempty (\(I\) is a member),
2307	  so the function \(J \mapsto v(f(P^I_J))\) attains a maximum at some \(J\). Since
2308	  \(P^I_I = 1\) (\cref{lem:gr_minorDet_self}) and \(v(1) = 1\), the value at \(I\)
2309	  is \(1\); hence the maximum is \(\ge 1\), so \(v(f(P^I_J)) \ne 0\) in \(\Gamma\),
2310	  i.e.\ \(f(P^I_J) \ne 0\). A square matrix over the field \(K\) with nonzero
2311	  determinant \(f(P^I_J) = \det f(X^I_J)\) is invertible.
2312	\end{proof}

2315	[E3 --- entries land in \(R\); \(g\) factors through the valuation ring]
2316	  \label{lem:gr_existence_factor_through_valuation_ring}

2336	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1.}
2337	  Define \(g : R^J = \mathbb{Z}[X^J] \to K\) by the matrix formula \(g(X^J) =
2338	  f\bigl((X^I_J)^{-1} X^I\bigr)\); concretely \(g = f' \circ \tilde\theta_{I,J}\),
2339	  where \(f' : R^I_J \to K\) is the extension of \(f\) to the away-localisation
2340	  \(R^I_J = \mathbb{Z}[X^I, 1/P^I_J]\) (well-defined by the universal property
2341	  \cref{lem:mathlib_away_lift}, since \(f(P^I_J)\) is a unit of \(K\) by
2342	  \cref{lem:gr_existence_minimal_valuation}). Then every value \(g(P^J_K)\) lies in
2343	  \(R\), and consequently \(g\) factors uniquely as \(g = (R \hookrightarrow K)
2344	  \circ g'\) for a ring homomorphism \(g' : R^J \to R\). Moreover \(g\) defines the
2345	  same \(K\)-point as \(f\).
2346	\end{lemma}

2348	  \uses{lem:gr_transitionPreMap_minorDet_mul, lem:gr_existence_minimal_valuation,
2349	    lem:gr_transitionPreMap_minorDet, lem:gr_isUnit_incl_transitionPreMap_cross}
2350	  Applying \(f'\) to the minor-ratio identity
2351	  (\cref{lem:gr_transitionPreMap_minorDet_mul}), and using that \(f'\) extends
2352	  \(f\), gives for every size-\(d\) subset \(K'\)
2353	  \[
2354	    g\bigl(P^J_{K'}\bigr)\cdot f(P^I_J) \;=\; f(P^I_{K'}),
2355	    \qquad\text{so}\qquad
2356	    g\bigl(P^J_{K'}\bigr) \;=\; \frac{f(P^I_{K'})}{f(P^I_J)} .
2357	  \]
2358	  By the maximality in \cref{lem:gr_existence_minimal_valuation},
2359	  \(v(f(P^I_{K'})) \le v(f(P^I_J))\), hence \(v\bigl(g(P^J_{K'})\bigr) =
2360	  v(f(P^I_{K'}))/v(f(P^I_J)) \le 1\), i.e.\ \(g(P^J_{K'}) \in R\). (That
2361	  \(f(P^I_J)\) is a unit and the cross minors map to units is recorded by
2362	  \cref{lem:gr_isUnit_incl_transitionPreMap_cross}, via the determinant identity of
2363	  \cref{lem:gr_transitionPreMap_minorDet}.) Now the \(J\)-minor \(X^J_J\) of \(X^J\)
2364	  is the identity, so cofactor expansion expresses each free entry \(x^J_{p,q}\)
2365	  (\(q \notin J\)) as, up to sign, the minor \(P^J_{K'}\) with \(K' = (J \setminus
2366	  \{j_p\}) \cup \{q\}\) --- the determinant obtained by replacing the \(p\)-th
2367	  identity column of \(X^J_J\) by column \(q\). Hence \(g(x^J_{p,q}) = \pm
2368	  g(P^J_{K'}) \in R\), while the entries with \(q \in J\) are \(0\) or \(1\),
2369	  already in \(R\). (This entry-as-minor sign bookkeeping is the one sub-step with
2370	  no pre-existing matrix-algebra scaffold; it requires expanding the determinant of
2371	  a column-substituted identity matrix.) Since \(g\) sends every generator of
2372	  \(\mathbb{Z}[X^J]\) into \(R\), it factors uniquely through the subring \(R
2373	  \subseteq K\) as \(g = (R \hookrightarrow K) \circ g'\) with \(g' : R^J \to R\).
2374	\end{proof}

3688	  Let \(j : U \hookrightarrow X\) be an open immersion of schemes and let \(M\) be a sheaf of modules
3689	  on \(X\). The global sections of the pullback module along \(j\) are canonically isomorphic to the
3690	  sections of \(M\) over the image of \(j\):
3691	  \[
3692	    \Gamma\bigl((\mathrm{pullback}\,j).\mathrm{obj}\,M,\ \top\bigr)
3693	    \;\cong\; \Gamma\bigl(M,\ \mathrm{range}\,j\bigr),
3694	  \]
3695	  naturally in the open argument: for opens \(V \le \top\) of \(U\) the comparison
3696	  \(\Gamma((\mathrm{pullback}\,j).\mathrm{obj}\,M, V) \cong \Gamma(M, j(V))\) intertwines the
3697	  restriction maps of the two presheaves, so any two such instances commute with the restriction
3698	  between them.

3705	  \(\Gamma(j^{\mathrm{res}} M, V) = \Gamma(M, j(V))\) \emph{definitionally}
3706	  (\cref{lem:modules_restrictFunctor_mathlib}); in particular at \(V = \top\) the right-hand side is
3707	  \(\Gamma(M, j(\top)) = \Gamma(M, \mathrm{range}\,j)\). The restriction functor is naturally
3708	  isomorphic to the universal-property pullback,
3709	  \(j^{\mathrm{res}} \cong j^{*} = \mathrm{pullback}\,j\)
3710	  (\cref{lem:modules_restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib},
3711	  \cref{lem:modules_pullback_mathlib}). Applying the global-sections functor \(\Gamma(-, V)\) to the
3712	  component of this natural isomorphism at \(M\) gives, for each open \(V\), an isomorphism
3713	  \(\Gamma((\mathrm{pullback}\,j).\mathrm{obj}\,M, V) \cong \Gamma(j^{\mathrm{res}} M, V)
3714	  = \Gamma(M, j(V))\); naturality of the functor isomorphism in \(V\) makes these commute with the
3715	  presheaf restriction maps. Taking \(V = \top\) yields the displayed isomorphism with
3716	  \(\Gamma(M, \mathrm{range}\,j)\).
3717	\end{proof}

# Strategy-Critic Directive — iter-037 (Quot-Foundations)

You are the fresh-context strategy critic. Read the strategy below as a fresh mathematician would.
Challenge structural soundness, sunk cost, missing prerequisites, hallucinated routes. You have NO
iter-by-iter history — that is intentional.

## Project goal (one paragraph)

Close the `sorry`-bearing nodes of the **Čech-independent leg** of the parent's
`thm:fga_pic_representability` cone (Kleiman, "The Picard scheme", FGA §4): (FBC) the i=0 flat base
change map `g^* f_* F ⟶ f'_* g'^* F` is an iso (Stacks 02KH); (GF) generic flatness with algebraic core
(Nitsure §4); (QUOT) Hilbert polynomial, Quot functor, Grassmannian scheme + representability (Nitsure
§1/§5). End-state: zero project `sorry` in the cone, kernel-only axioms. Names/labels are the parent's so
